Is there any danger of spinning in this loop if the module isn't loaded?  Should there be a short sleep after request_module() to give it a chance to initialize?

Patch Set 2: Code-Review+1

I'm not against that patch, but our workflow always unsure this module is loaded before we mount the filesystem, so this is not a path for the bugs we are seeing.

Patch Set 2:

Trevis Autotest 2 retesting enforced custom session on el9.3-x86_64/ldiskfs servers and clients for build 104172 patch 2. Requested by Andreas Dilger because of LU-17741.
The following test parameters were specified: kerberos=true testlist=sanity-krb5.

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2: Code-Review+1
> 
> I'm not against that patch, but our workflow always unsure this module is loaded before we mount the filesystem, so this is not a path for the bugs we are seeing.

Interesting. In this case, the culprit could be the call to try_module_get(). Let me work on a different patch, I need to find a way to make sure try_module_get() is called only once per import.

Patch Set 2:

Looks good to me.

Abandoned

Not proved to be really useful.

commit.subject: LU-17742 gss: fix ptlrpc_gss ref count
commit.message:
LU-17742 gss: fix ptlrpc_gss ref count

Make sure we do not call request_module on ptlrpc_gss multiple times.

Fixes: b80d6defb7 ("LU-16888 gss: fix ptlrpc_gss automatic loading")
Test-Parameters: trivial
Test-Parameters: testgroup=review-dne-selinux-ssk-part-2
Test-Parameters: kerberos=true testlist=sanity-krb5
Signed-off-by: Sebastien Buisson <sbuisson@ddn.com>
Change-Id: If7a8ef8899d00fdcb7c5df476a68e761e14e5b16
